data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture to improve scalability and isolate services for individual teams.
data.technical_spec: Distributed system composed of independently deployable services.

data.title: AWS Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: The platform runs on Amazon’s own AWS infrastructure, providing cloud-based service resilience.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS EC2, S3, and other cloud services to host applications.

data.title: Data Storage
data.description: Various storage solutions are used for different purposes, including Amazon RDS and DynamoDB.
data.technical_spec: Relational and NoSQL databases to manage transactional and big data needs.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Amazon uses CloudFront to deliver content rapidly to global users.
data.technical_spec: Edge locations for low-latency data delivery.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: To process and respond to events quickly, leveraging services like AWS Lambda.
data.technical_spec: Serverless computing to handle real-time data feeds and processing.
